pi_items.source: Multiple authoritative sources (imagej.net, bioimagebook.github.io, petebankhead.gitbooks.io) all reference this as the exact menu label in File > Open Samples. Confirmed by 4 existing tasks in this repository that use the same sample.

pi_items.metadata_value: Fiji built-in sample (File > Open Samples > Mitosis (26MB, 5D stack))
pi_items.verified_value: Fiji built-in sample (File > Open Samples > Mitosis (26MB, 5D stack))
pi_items.source: Confirmed by ImageJ User Guide (imagej.net/ij/docs/guide/146-8.html), Fiji Introduction presentation (imagej.github.io/presentations/2017-05/fiji-introduction), and Introduction to Bioimage Analysis textbook (bioimagebook.github.io). Also confirmed by 4 existing tasks in this repository (temporal_change_detection, hyperstack_z_projection_profiling, multicell_timeseries_extraction, kymograph_motility_analysis) which all use this same File > Open Samples path.

Verified via web search: DeepWiki ImageJ documentation (deepwiki.com/imagej/ImageJ/3.3-image-stacks-and-hyperstacks) and multiple tutorial sources confirm 171x196, 2C, 5Z, 51T. The existing task mitosis_timepoint_analysis/task.json metadata INCORRECTLY claims '51 z-slices, 5 time frames' (Z and T swapped), but the temporal_change_detection task description references '51 time frames' which is correct. The dimension order is XYCZT.

This is an output filename chosen by the task designer, not a data claim. The filename matches the task description. After Z-projecting one channel across Z with 'All time frames' enabled, the result is a 171x196 TIFF with 51 frames.
Output filename chosen by task designer. Same dimensions as dna_projection (171x196, 51 frames) after Z-projection of the second channel.
Output filename for ROI Manager save. ImageJ ROI Manager saves ROI sets as ZIP files containing individual .roi files. The ROI Manager More > Save... command produces this format.
Output filename for Multi Measure results. Multi Measure with 'Measure all slices' produces a Results table with N_ROIs * N_slices rows. File > Save As from the Results window saves as CSV.
Output filename for temporal montage. Image > Stacks > Make Montage with columns=6, rows=1 on a 6-frame substack produces a single-row montage saved as TIFF.

Confirmed by examining setup_task.sh which creates this directory with mkdir -p and chown ga:ga. Consistent with all 68 other imagej_env tasks which use the same output directory path. The env.json mounts assets at /home/ga/ImageJ_Data with mode rw.

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly claimed in metadata, but task description implies one channel is DNA/chromosomes
pi_items.verified_value: C1/Red channel shows chromosomes/DNA. When Split Channels is run, C1-mitosis.tif is displayed with a Red LUT.
pi_items.source: 3 out of 4 existing tasks in this repository that use the Mitosis sample describe the Red/C1 channel as 'chromosomes' (hyperstack_z_projection_profiling: 'chromosomes (Channel 1/Red)', kymograph_motility_analysis: 'Red channel (chromosomes)'). One task (multicell_timeseries_extraction) calls it 'Red channel (Microtubules)' which is inconsistent. The Image5D documentation on imagej.net describes a related H2B-GFP mitosis sample as having 'fluorescence and transmission' channels, but that is a different dataset (24 z-slices, 7 frames vs 5 z-slices, 51 frames).

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly claimed in metadata, but task description implies one channel is tubulin/spindle
pi_items.verified_value: C2/Green channel — described as 'spindles' by temporal_change_detection task, but may be transmitted light rather than tubulin fluorescence. Exact biological content uncertain.
pi_items.source: Only 1 existing task (temporal_change_detection) uses C2/Green and calls it 'Green channel (spindles)'. The agent successfully processed this channel and scored 85/100 VLM, confirming it shows visible structures. However, the Image5D documentation describes a related mitosis sample as having 'fluorescence and transmission' (not two fluorescence channels). The exact content of C2 in the Fiji Mitosis sample could not be independently confirmed from authoritative documentation. The analysis workflow works regardless of whether C2 is tubulin fluorescence or transmitted light.

privileged_info_summary: The Fiji Mitosis (26MB, 5D stack) sample is a real confocal time-lapse of HeLa cells. It has dimensions 171x196 pixels, 2 channels, 5 Z-slices, and 51 time frames (XYCZT order). After Image > Color > Split Channels, two images are produced: C1-mitosis.tif (Red LUT, shows chromosomes/DNA) and C2-mitosis.tif (Green LUT, shows spindle-like structures). After Image > Stacks > Z Project with 'Max Intensity' and 'All time frames' checked, each channel becomes a 171x196 time series with 51 frames. Nuclear segmentation on the DNA channel at timepoint 1 using Gaussian Blur sigma=2, Otsu threshold, Fill Holes, Watershed, and Analyze Particles (size >= 100 px², exclude edges) should yield a small number of nuclei (the field of view is limited, expect roughly 2-8 objects depending on exact threshold). The ROI set from Analyze Particles with 'Add to Manager' is saved via ROI Manager > More > Save as a ZIP containing .roi files. Multi Measure with 'Measure all slices' on the tubulin projection produces N_ROIs * 51 rows. The CSV should contain columns including Mean (and Area, IntDen if Set Measurements was configured). A temporal montage of frames 1,11,21,31,41,51 using Image > Stacks > Tools > Make Substack then Image > Stacks > Make Montage (columns=6, rows=1) produces an image approximately 1026 pixels wide (6*171) and 196 pixels tall. NOTE: The existing task mitosis_timepoint_analysis has Z and T dimensions SWAPPED in its metadata (claims '51 z-slices, 5 time frames' when the correct dimensions are 5 z-slices, 51 time frames). Our metadata is correct.
